findings.summary: AppErrors are rethrown across boundaries without adding the same context/logging as unknown errors.
evidence: In `createTask`, `isAppError(err)` is rethrown directly (`src/shared/lib/taskCreation/createTask.ts:88`), while unknown errors go through `handleAndRethrow` with boundary `logData` (`src/shared/lib/taskCreation/createTask.ts:92`).

The same split exists in both task wrappers: direct AppError passthrough (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageUpscale.ts:103`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/videoEnhance.ts:243`) vs contextual `handleAndRethrow` for non-AppError (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageUpscale.ts:107`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/videoEnhance.ts:247`).
`handleAndRethrow` always routes to `handleError` (normalize + log) (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts:42`), so observability differs by error category rather than operation boundary.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a boundary helper used in all three creators that enriches both known AppErrors and unknown errors with the same operation context. For example: if `isAppError(err)`, append boundary metadata before rethrow; otherwise normalize/log via `handleAndRethrow` with that same metadata.
findings.reasoning: Failures from the same operation currently produce different context/log richness depending on whether the error is already an AppError. This inconsistency makes debugging and telemetry correlation less reliable.
